lines.english: Nor the seven realms beneath the earth, shall remain.
Neither the just, nor the generous, nor any humans at all,
First Mehl:
You alone, Lord, You alone. ||2||
The One Lord alone exists. Who else is there?
Neither the sun, nor the moon, nor the planets,
First Mehl:
You alone, Lord, You alone. ||3||
Nor the seven continents, nor the oceans,
Nor food, nor the wind-nothing is permanent.
Our sustenance is not in the hands of any person.
First Mehl:
You alone, Lord, You alone. ||4||
The hopes of all rest in the One Lord.
First Mehl:
The birds have no money in their pockets.
The One Lord alone exists-who else is there?
You alone, Lord, You alone. ||5||
He alone is the Giver.
O Nanak, that destiny which is pre-ordained and written on one's forehead
You alone, Lord, You alone. ||6||
They place their hopes on trees and water.
First Mehl:
You alone, O Lord, You alone. ||7||
no one can erase it.
The Lord infuses strength, and He takes it away again.
True is the Hukam of Your Command. To the Gurmukh, it is known.
Pauree:
Through the Guru's Teachings, selfishness and conceit are eradicated, and the Truth is realized.
True is Your Court. It is proclaimed and revealed through the Word of the Shabad.
The self-willed manmukhs are always false; they are deluded by doubt.

lines.gurmukhi: ਵਿਸਟਾ ਅੰਦਰਿ ਵਾਸੁ ਸਾਦੁ ਨ ਜਾਣਿਆ ॥
lines.translit: visattaa andar vaas saad na jaaniaa |
lines.english: They dwell in manure, and they do not know the taste of the Name.
Meditating deeply on the True Word of the Shabad, I have merged into the Truth.

lines.gurmukhi: ਵਿਣੁ ਨਾਵੈ ਦੁਖੁ ਪਾਇ ਆਵਣ ਜਾਣਿਆ ॥
lines.translit: vin naavai dukh paae aavan jaaniaa |
lines.english: Without the Name, they suffer the agonies of coming and going.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਪਾਰਖੁ ਆਪਿ ਜਿਨਿ ਖੋਟਾ ਖਰਾ ਪਛਾਣਿਆ ॥੧੩॥
lines.translit: naanak paarakh aap jin khottaa kharaa pachhaaniaa |13|
lines.english: O Nanak, the Lord Himself is the Appraiser, who distinguishes the counterfeit from the genuine. ||13||
Salok, First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੀਹਾ ਬਾਜਾ ਚਰਗਾ ਕੁਹੀਆ ਏਨਾ ਖਵਾਲੇ ਘਾਹ ॥
lines.translit: seehaa baajaa charagaa kuheea enaa khavaale ghaah |
lines.english: Tigers, hawks, falcons and eagles-the Lord could make them eat grass.

lines.gurmukhi: ਘਾਹੁ ਖਾਨਿ ਤਿਨਾ ਮਾਸੁ ਖਵਾਲੇ ਏਹਿ ਚਲਾਏ ਰਾਹ ॥
lines.translit: ghaahu khaan tinaa maas khavaale ehi chalaae raah |
lines.english: And those animals which eat grass-He could make them eat meat. He could make them follow this way of life.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕੀੜਾ ਥਾਪਿ ਦੇਇ ਪਾਤਿਸਾਹੀ ਲਸਕਰ ਕਰੇ ਸੁਆਹ ॥
lines.translit: keerraa thaap dee paatisaahee lasakar kare suaah |
lines.english: He could appoint a worm as king, and reduce an army to ashes.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਦੀਆ ਵਿਚਿ ਟਿਬੇ ਦੇਖਾਲੇ ਥਲੀ ਕਰੇ ਅਸਗਾਹ ॥
lines.translit: nadeea vich ttibe dekhaale thalee kare asagaah |
lines.english: He could raise dry land from the rivers, and turn the deserts into bottomless oceans.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਜਿਉ ਜਿਉ ਸਚੇ ਭਾਵੈ ਤਿਉ ਤਿਉ ਦੇਇ ਗਿਰਾਹ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: naanak jiau jiau sache bhaavai tiau tiau dee giraah |1|
lines.english: O Nanak, as it pleases the True Lord, He gives us sustenance. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੇਤੇ ਜੀਅ ਜੀਵਹਿ ਲੈ ਸਾਹਾ ਜੀਵਾਲੇ ਤਾ ਕਿ ਅਸਾਹ ॥
lines.translit: jete jeea jeeveh lai saahaa jeevaale taa ki asaah |
lines.english: All beings and creatures live by breathing, but He could keep us alive, even without the breath.
First Mehl:
Some eat meat, while others eat grass.
Some have all the thirty-six varieties of delicacies,
while others live in the dirt and eat mud.
Some live by the Support of the Naam, the Name of the Formless Lord.
Some control the breath, and regulate their breathing.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਮੁਠੇ ਜਾਹਿ ਨਾਹੀ ਮਨਿ ਸੋਇ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: naanak mutthe jaeh naahee man soe |2|
lines.english: O Nanak, those who do not enshrine the Lord within their minds are deluded. ||2||
Pauree:
The Great Giver lives; no one dies.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੂਰੇ ਗੁਰ ਕੀ ਕਾਰ ਕਰਮਿ ਕਮਾਈਐ ॥
lines.translit: poore gur kee kaar karam kamaaeeai |
lines.english: By the karma of good actions, some come to serve the Perfect Guru.
Through the Guru's Teachings, some eliminate selfishness and conceit, and meditate on the Naam, the Name of the Lord.
Undertaking any other task, they waste their lives in vain.

lines.gurmukhi: ਵਿਣੁ ਨਾਵੈ ਸਭ ਵਿਸੁ ਪੈਝੈ ਖਾਈਐ ॥
lines.translit: vin naavai sabh vis paijhai khaaeeai |
lines.english: Without the Name, all that they wear and eat is poison.

lines.gurmukhi: ਵਿਣੁ ਸਤਿਗੁਰੁ ਸੇਵੇ ਨਾਹੀ ਸੁਖਿ ਨਿਵਾਸੁ ਫਿਰਿ ਫਿਰਿ ਆਈਐ ॥
lines.translit: vin satigur seve naahee sukh nivaas fir fir aaeeai |
lines.english: Without serving the True Guru, they do not obtain the home of peace; they are consigned to reincarnation, over and over again.
Praising the True Word of the Shabad, they merge with the True Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਸਚੁ ਖਰਾ ਸਾਲਾਹਿ ਪਤਿ ਸਿਉ ਜਾਈਐ ॥੧੪॥
lines.translit: naanak sach kharaa saalaeh pat siau jaaeeai |14|
lines.english: O Nanak, singing the Praises of the Pure, True Lord, they depart with honor. ||14||
Investing counterfeit capital, they earn only falsehood in the world.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੁਧੁ ਭਾਵੈ ਤਾ ਵਾਵਹਿ ਗਾਵਹਿ ਤੁਧੁ ਭਾਵੈ ਜਲਿ ਨਾਵਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: tudh bhaavai taa vaaveh gaaveh tudh bhaavai jal naaveh |
lines.english: When it pleases You, we play music and sing; when it pleases You, we bathe in water.
Salok, First Mehl:
